sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Empoli and Parma at the Carlo Castellani stadium in Empoli promises to be a critical encounter for both sides. Empoli, sitting in 19th place with just 25 points, are in desperate need of a win to climb out of the relegation zone. Meanwhile, Parma, positioned 15th with 32 points, will look to solidify their mid-table status with a positive result. With both teams having something to prove, this match could be a tightly contested battle.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, it’s a closely fought history with 2 wins for the home team, 2 draws, and 1 win for the away side. Their most recent encounter on October 27, 2024, ended in a 1-1 draw with Gabriel Charpentier earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.6. Empoli have remained unbeaten in their last 3 head-to-head games against Parma, giving them a slight psychological edge going into this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Empoli are in dire straits, currently on a streak of 3 consecutive losses and without a win in their last 11 matches. They have also failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 5 games and have been the first to concede in 8 of their last 10 matches. On the other hand, Parma have had mixed results, with their last 5 away games seeing under 2.5 goals in 5 out of 7 matches. Both teams have shown a trend of fewer than 10.5 corners in their recent games, with Empoli at 10/10 and Parma at 6/7, suggesting a low-corner game might be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Empoli
sections.text: For Empoli, forward Sebastiano Esposito stands out with 8 goals scored this season and an impressive expected goals (xG) overperformance of 2.542, making him a constant threat in attack. Defender Mattia De Sciglio, ranked 5th in the league for aerial duels won at 75%, will be crucial in defending against Parma’s aerial threats. Additionally, midfielder Giuseppe Pezzella, ranked 4th league-wide for big chances created with 10, could be the creative spark Empoli need to break down Parma’s defense. Young defender Luca Marianucci, with a league rank of 2 for long balls in total passes at 10.7%, might also play a role in switching play effectively.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Parma
sections.text: Parma’s attack will likely be led by forward Ange-Yoan Bonny, who has 6 goals and 3 assists this season despite an xG underperformance of -2.9954, showing room for improvement. Defender Enrico Del Prato has been a surprising goal-scoring threat with 4 goals and a strong xG overperformance of 1.9397, making him dangerous from set pieces. Midfielder Dennis Man, with 4 goals and 4 assists, will be key in linking play, while Milan Đurić, who has a history of performing well against Empoli with an average Sofascore rating of 7.1 and 1 goal in 4 starts, could be a decisive factor.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - Empoli
sections.text: Empoli’s season statistics highlight their struggles, with only 27 goals scored (18th in the league) and 55 goals conceded (17th for least goals conceded). Their average ball possession of 40.6% ranks them 19th, showing difficulty in controlling games, while they lead the league in fouls committed with 523. However, they excel defensively in certain areas, ranking 1st in tackles per game (18.657), interceptions per game (8.714), and clearances per game (26.829). Interestingly, Empoli’s goal distribution shows a late surge, with 29.09% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - Parma
sections.text: Parma have shown a balanced approach in their goal-scoring distribution, with a significant 25.93% of their goals coming in the final 15 minutes of matches (76th to 90th minutes), suggesting they often finish strongly. Their forward line has been bolstered by consistent contributions, though they miss key players like Nahuel Estévez, ranked 1st league-wide for aerial duels won at 82.4%. Parma’s recent form player, Jacob Ondrejka, has been in fine shape with an average Sofascore rating of 7.65 and 3 goals in his last 5 matches, potentially posing a threat off the bench.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Referee Michael Fabbri, who oversees this match, averages 4.8 yellow cards per game and 0.26 red cards per game, indicating a likelihood of bookings in this fixture. Empoli have accumulated 63 yellow cards this season (6th least in the league) with an average of 1.8 per game, while their head-to-head stats with Parma show less than 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 8 encounters. In terms of corners, both teams have a history of low-corner games, with Empoli at 4.2 corners per game (14th in the league) and a trend of under 10.5 corners in all their last 10 matches.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Empoli will feel the absence of forward Lorenzo Colombo, who has scored 5 goals this season, and Christian Kouamé, with 1 goal and 1 assist, weakening their attacking options. Parma, on the other hand, will miss midfielder Adrián Bernabé, who has an average Sofascore rating of 7.14, and forward Matteo Cancellieri with 3 goals, which could impact their midfield creativity and forward thrust. These absences might tilt the balance slightly, depending on how both teams adapt tactically.
